results.title: A Special place
results.creator_credit: Bill Pettigrew, Bill Brind and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: An absorbing, eye-opening view of Canada’s National Parks, illustrated by the activities of the parks staff - naturalists, geologists, park wardens and others who ensure that these special places remain that way. Mountain rescue, avalanche control, health services to animals, information service to visitors, restocking fishing waters - these and many other responsibilities occupy the National Parks officers. It is a view of the Canadian wilderness that encourages understanding and confidence.

results.title: A Song for Tibet
results.creator_credit: Anne Henderson, Abbey Jack Neidik, Kent Martin, Arcady Films, D L I Productions and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Two Tibetans-in-exile, living in Canada tell of their efforts to preserve the culture and religion of Tibet under the occupation. They journey to India to meet the Dali Lama. Narrated by Thubten Samdup and Dicki Chhoyang.

results.title: A Sense of touch
results.creator_credit: Don White, George Johnson, John Taylor and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: The evocative images in this film stimulate associations between visual and tactile information. Vivid close-ups of sensuous activities such as licking a popsicle, stroking a beard and walking barefoot in the grass encourage the viewer to explore the tactile properties of objects using different parts of the body. The sound track consists of music without words, which provides pacing and highlights the nature of different sensory experiences.

results.title: A Sense of sound
results.creator_credit: Don White, George Johnson, John Taylor and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: A Sense of Sound illustrates relationships among sight, hearing and other senses as sources of information about the world. A kaleidoscope of everyday visual images, accompanied by a sometimes realistic, sometimes playful sound track, shows how sound communicates messages and stimulates associations with past experiences. The juxtaposition of sensible and nonsense associations encourages the viewer to examine how we interpret auditory messages.

results.title: A Sense of smell
results.brief_description: Born in Paris, France and emigrating to Sydney, Australia as a child with her recently divorced mum, writer director Throsby in this autobiographical film, Sense of Smell explores the connection between scent and memory. Being in a vastly different cultural environment Throsby recalls how the smell of the cakes in the local European cake store in Sydney enabled her to vividly recapture her earlier childhood memories of Paris, transporting her to her birthplace helping her form a sense of place. In trying to develop a picture of place and memory Throsby goes to Paris and talks with Parisians who share their stories to discover that ‘smell’ is the key to her memories.

results.title: A Sense of place
results.creator_credit: Rene Bonniere, Roman Bittman and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Deals with the mass movement of people from rural to urban areas, and the solutions being sought by the world’s governments. There are many reasons behind this migration, but the main one is hope for a better life. Shows man’s resourcefulness in coping with enormous problems and the desperate need to cope with change.

results.title: A Sense of humus
results.creator_credit: Christopher Chapman, Gary Toole, Roman Bittman and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: This film is about organic farming done on a commercial basis. The farmers, who live in various parts of Canada, discuss their methods, and the audience is able to appreciate the results obtained. A pertinent and reassuring study of agriculture in the age of chemicals and mechanization.

results.title: A Selection of early films: 1896-1913
results.creator_credit: Cecil Hepworth, Edwin S. Porter, Emile Cohl, Ferdinand Zecca, Georges Melies, Louis Lumiere and British Film Institute
results.brief_description: This selection is designed to illustrate the main stages in the development of the cinema in Britain from the first public performance in 1896 to 1913, when the full-length feature film became firmly established. Titles featured in this compilation are: ‘Train entering a station’ by Lumiere; ‘The Indiarubber head’ by Melies; ‘Queen Victoria’s funeral’; ‘The Revolving table’ by Hepworth; ‘Answering a call’, ‘The Burning stable’ by Hepworth, ‘The Great Train robbery’ by Porter; ‘The Well washed house’ by Zecca; ‘Drama among the puppets’ by Cohl; with extracts from ‘Simple Charity’ by Griffiths and ‘Dante’s Inferno’ and East Lynne.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Cecil Hepworth, Edwin S. Porter, Emile Cohl, Ferdinand Zecca, Georges Melies, Louis Lumiere and British Film Institute

results.title: A Search for learning
results.creator_credit: Donald Shebib, Joseph Koenig and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: A film for teachers, describing the use of the “discovery method” in teaching. All it needs is a teacher whose encouragement is natural and unobtrusive. The film shows a free environment where even the furniture can be arranged to meet the needs of a particular inquiry. Film loops and other visual media are shown being used to advantage in this method of teaching.

results.title: A Short film on solar energy
results.creator_credit: Elaine Bass, Saul Bass, Michael Britton, Warner Communications and Wild Wood Enterprises
results.brief_description: A lighthearted film which uses animation to trace man’s use of energy through history to the oil shortage crises of the present, and the solutions solar power can offer. From the series “Consumer action now”.
